scope: Covers nanoscience and nanotechnology including synthesis, characterization, self-assembly, nanomaterials properties, and nanotechnology applications.
Covers medicinal chemistry including drug design, structure-activity relationships, ADMET, chemical biology, and drug discovery.
Covers chemical biology including bioorthogonal chemistry, chemical probes, protein engineering, and chemical approaches to biology.
Covers analytical chemistry including new methods for chemical measurement, instrumentation, biosensors, and analytical applications.
Covers synthetic biology including genetic circuits, metabolic engineering, cell-free systems, protein engineering, and CRISPR applications.

Some journals have undergone title or publisher changes during their lifetimes. For more information about individual title history, please visit the journal's list of issues page on pubs.acs.org and look in the "former titles" information box.

The ACS All Publications, Core Plus, and Science Essentials Packages provide front-file access only.  For access to prior content, a separate subscription to the ACS Legacy Archives is needed.  Exceptions where subscription access includes all back issues applies to Journal of Chemical Education, ACS Chemical Health & Safety, and Journal of the American Society for Mass Spectrometry.
This journal is gold open access and free to access but should be added to the library discovery systems.
This is the online edition of C&EN news magazine that is made available to organizational subscribers. It is included in most, but not all, ACS All Publications and ACS Core Plus Packages.

Starting in 1997, the Journal of Physical Chemistry split into two sections, A and B.  In 2007 a third, C, was introduced.  Editorially, these are treated as three separate journals. Transactionally, all three sections (A, B, and C) are treated as a single journal subscription.
This journal ceased publishing new articles in 2021. Previously published content will continue to be available.
Fee waivers may be available for publishing authors. Visit https://acsopenscience.org/researchers/oa-pricing/#discounts and select "Other fully open access journals" to view possible waivers.
